hypothesis: Combining MFI momentum with Vortex signal confirmation and EMA trend direction filters out whipsaws and improves signal reliability, while ADX ensures trend strength.

Combining Vortex bullish confirmation with EMA/MFI momentum divergence and volatility filtering should improve signal quality and reduce false entries, while volume filtering at exit prevents whipsaws.
Combining EMA, MFI, and Vortex aims to capture momentum shifts confirmed by directional changes and volume, while gating by implied volatility reduces noise and whipsaws.
Combining MFI momentum with Vortex signal and EMA crossover aims to capture trend reversals with confirmation, while ADX filters out whipsaws during periods of low trend strength.
